# Preface {.unnumbered}

## Vision {.unnumbered}
To build a "Walking Skeleton" for to be able to display different kind of data that can be tabularized. 
The Dashboard is aimed at being modular, scalable, resusable and easy to maintain. 

### Our first goal {.unnumbered}

Our first goal is to build a multi-species dashboard, to display results of bioinformatic analyses from pipelines hosted on different platforms.

The first implementation starts for pipelines hosted on the following platforms:
- on "Use Galaxy" based platform
- local "IRIDA" based platform
- Or to allow user to upload results produced by galaxy pipelines and visualize them on the dashboard. 

As prototyping option we chose: 
- User upload results produced from a pipeline and associated metadata

- **Shiny (Express) for Python** for UI and Interactivity.
- **Polars LazyFrames** for high-performance, out-of-core data handling.
- **Plotnine** for publication-quality, grammar-of-graphics visualizations.
- **Project/Collection Paradigm (ADR-003)**: Decouples domain logic from software architecture using universal terminology.

### Integrity & Trust {.unnumbered}
As of **April 2026**, the system has achieved **100% Test Integrity**:
- **Transformer**: 60/60 registered actions verified via atomic integrity suites (62 total, 2 relational tested via assembly).
- **Viz Factory**: 172/172 components validated via the 1:1:1 evidence loop (TSV/YAML/PNG).
- **Clean-Room Verification**: All system outputs are reproducible using library-native CLI runners.

### Filter Message Flow

::: {.lightbox}
```{mermaid}
{{< include foundations/_sequence_data_flow.mmd >}}
```
:::

### Our vision of the feature {.unnumbered}

We hoped that this work will not only be a deliverable of a short-term project, but will be able to grow into a long-term, maintainable solution. 

Therefore we paid particular attention to the architecture of the app, and the separation of concerns between different components, 
attempting to build a professional, scalable app. 

We adopted a **Layered Architecture**, allowing us to separate the concerns of data ingestion, data wrangling, visualization logic, and the dashboard app itself. The **Decoupling approach** of the different aspects of the app should improve layer reusability, maintainability while allowing for further extensions. 

It would also allow to swap to a different UI (user interface) or the Data source (e.g., move from CSVs to a SQL database) without rewriting the core visualization logic.

### 📚 User Resources {.unnumbered}

For analysts and non-developers navigating the platform:
- [**The Scientific Cookbook**](./appendix/user_guide_gallery.qmd): A guide to the Gallery and sharing research "recipes."
- [**The Trunk and Branch Guide**](./reference/wrangling_guide.qmd): Understanding the Tiered Wrangling and assembly logic.
- [**Developing without Code**](./appendix/dev_studio_rationale.qmd): Understanding the Dev Studio and Building Block analogies.
- [**The Data Lifecycle**](./appendix/data_lifecycle_theater.qmd): A deep dive into Tiers 1-3 and the Comparison Theater.

### 🚀 Admin & Deployment Resources {.unnumbered}

For system administrators deploying SPARMVET in production:
- [**Deployment Guide**](./deployment/deployment_guide.qmd): How to configure the app for Galaxy, IRIDA, institutional servers, and local machines using a deployment profile YAML.
- [**UI Personas**](./workflows/ui_persona.qmd): What each persona can do, the full feature flag matrix, and how personas interact with deployment profiles.
